summary: Matched several previously incomplete or partially matched camera routines and tightened camera/stage interfaces. The PR added real implementations for Camera_8002928C and Camera_8002AF68, improved matching for Camera_80029AAC and Camera_8002A28C, corrected Camera_80029C88's signature to include an unused CameraBounds parameter, exposed CameraBox bitfields and a cooldown-like s16 field, added a Camera unk_2ba field, and typed several stage-specific camera eye-y-bound helpers as float-returning functions. It also named SHRINEROUTE and HOMERUN internal stage IDs so camera code could switch on them directly.
what_changed: Implemented Camera_8002928C as a bool-returning camera-box state check using Stage_GetCamBoundsLeftOffset, Stage_GetCamBoundsRightOffset, Stage_GetCamBoundsTopOffset, and Stage_GetCamBoundsBottomOffset, with a small inline cam_bound helper testing normalized coordinates outside 0.35f..0.65f.

Changed CameraBox layout in src/melee/cm/types.h from one xC_b0 bitfield plus padding to xC_b0, xC_b1, xC_b2 bitfields and s16 xE, allowing Camera_80028F5C and Camera_8002928C to address individual flags and a timer/counter directly.
Updated Camera_80028F5C initialization to clear xC_b0, xC_b1, xC_b2 as boolean bitfields and set xE to 0 instead of masking bits and writing to padding.

Reworked Camera_80029AAC with clearer dx/dy calculations, a CameraUnkGlobals pointer to cm_803BCCA0, and direct incremental interest updates; the diff indicates this was a matching-oriented rewrite rather than a behavioral feature change.

Changed Camera_80029C88 signature from void Camera_80029C88(CameraMovement*, f32) to void Camera_80029C88(CameraBounds*, CameraMovement*, f32), preserving an unused first parameter and removing a previous todo about swapped r3/r4 arguments.

Rewrote Camera_8002A28C to access cm_80452C68 directly instead of through local Camera*, added a u8 stack pad, copied unk_B0 to unk_1B0, cleared source xC fields, decremented unk_8C timers, and unlinked unk_A0 through HSD_GObjPLink_80390228 when conditions match.

Implemented Camera_8002AF68 to set HSD_CObj FOV, interest, and eye position from CameraMovement plus cm_80452C68.translation, while clamping eye y to a stage-specific lower bound for CASTLE, CORNERIA, ZEBES, GARDEN, SHRINEROUTE, and HOMERUN.
Added includes in camera.c for grcastle, grcorneria, grzebes, grgarden, grshrineroute, grhomerun, and baselib/controller.h.
Typed grCastle_801D0FF0, grCorneria_801E2FCC, grZebes_801DCCC8, grGarden_80203624, grShrineRoute_802087B0, and grHomeRun_8021EF10 as float-returning void-argument functions in their stage headers.
Named InternalStageId entries SHRINEROUTE and HOMERUN, with HOMERUN explicitly assigned 0x43.
Adjusted Camera struct padding around offset 0x2BA to add s16 unk_2ba before f32 unk_2bc.
smart_moves: Converted hidden bit manipulation and padding access in CameraBox into explicit bitfields and xE, making later camera matching code easier to express without relying on anonymous fill bytes.

Preserved an unused first parameter in Camera_80029C88 to resolve the earlier register/order mismatch noted in the removed todo; this is a common decomp tactic when call ABI evidence implies an argument exists even if the function body does not use it.
Used direct global accesses to cm_80452C68 in Camera_8002A28C plus an explicit stack padding array to influence register allocation and stack shape for matching.
Localized the repeated camera-bound threshold test into a static inline cam_bound helper, keeping Camera_8002928C readable while still matching the simple comparison pattern.
Tied Camera_8002AF68 to stage-specific float helpers through properly typed prototypes instead of leaving those calls as UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS, improving both type safety and generated code consistency.
Used named internal stage IDs in the Camera_8002AF68 switch, reducing magic enum usage and documenting which stage-specific camera lower-bound functions participate in eye-position clamping.
decomp_lessons: When a field is used as individual bits in one function and as state flags in another, exposing actual bitfields can remove awkward masks and help match boolean assignments.
If a function has a persistent register mismatch, consider whether the true prototype contains an unused leading parameter rather than forcing argument swaps inside the body.
Small stack padding declarations such as u8 _[16] or u8 _1[4] can be useful matching tools when a function's logic is known but stack/register allocation is off.

Typing cross-module stage helper functions can unlock camera matches that depend on their return type; replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS with float(void) is meaningful when callers use the return as a numeric bound.
Stage enum naming can be driven by decompiled camera logic: if a switch calls grShrineRoute or grHomeRun helpers, naming those InternalStageId values makes the code more self-documenting.
Direct global references may produce closer codegen than holding a pointer local, even when a local pointer is cleaner C; matching sometimes requires sacrificing abstraction.
naming_conventions: Camera functions retain address-based names such as Camera_8002928C, Camera_80029AAC, Camera_8002A28C, and Camera_8002AF68.
Unknown CameraBox fields are named by offset and bit position, e.g. xC_b0, xC_b1, xC_b2, and xE.
Unknown Camera struct fields continue offset-based names such as unk_2ba and unk_2bc.
Internal stage enum names use all-caps stage identifiers such as CASTLE, CORNERIA, ZEBES, GARDEN, SHRINEROUTE, and HOMERUN.
Stage helper prototypes follow existing module/address naming, e.g. grCastle_801D0FF0 and grHomeRun_8021EF10, even after their return types are clarified.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Added an unused CameraBounds* parameter to Camera_80029C88 to align the ABI/register expectations; the removed comment explicitly said r3 and r4 needed to be swapped for a match.
Inserted explicit stack padding arrays in Camera_8002A28C and Camera_8002AF68, likely to reproduce target stack layout or register allocation.
Changed Camera_8002A28C from local Camera* pointer-based access to direct cm_80452C68 global member access to improve matching.
Reordered and simplified arithmetic in Camera_80029AAC, including computing dx and dy once and applying movement->interest.x += dx * var_f1 style updates.
Introduced a local CameraUnkGlobals* unk = &cm_803BCCA0 in Camera_80029AAC to shape repeated global field access codegen.
Used float-returning stage prototypes so Camera_8002AF68 can compare vec.y against eye_y_bound without unknown-return casts.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The only recorded human activity count was one issue comment, but its body was not included in the excerpt, so no reviewer intent or feedback can be inferred.
follow_up_queries: Find callers of Camera_80029C88 to verify all call sites use the added CameraBounds* argument consistently.
Search for CameraBox xC_b1, xC_b2, and xE usage to refine field names beyond offset-based placeholders.

Investigate stage helper functions grCastle_801D0FF0, grCorneria_801E2FCC, grZebes_801DCCC8, grGarden_80203624, grShrineRoute_802087B0, and grHomeRun_8021EF10 to determine semantic names for the returned eye-y lower bound.
Check whether Camera.unk_2ba is used elsewhere and whether it can be renamed or grouped with nearby camera timing/config fields.
Compare Camera_8002AF68 against remaining camera cobj setup functions to identify shared translation, interest, eye-position, and fov patterns.
